level_signals: 5+ years of experience as a Technical Program Manager or Engineering Program Manager
Proven track record of delivering complex, multi-workstream technical programs
Expected to own end-to-end delivery across multiple teams

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience as a Technical Program Manager or Engineering Program Manager, ideally with meaningful time spent supporting or embedded in an AI/ML Research organization
must_haves.qualifier: ideally with meaningful time spent supporting or embedded in an AI/ML Research organization

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: In-depth, working knowledge of how machine learning systems are built (i.e., data pipelines, model training, evaluation, and serving) that allows you to engage meaningfully with researchers and engineers on technical tradeoffs
Proven track record of delivering complex, multi-workstream technical programs on time across teams with competing priorities
Demonstrated ability to map cross-functional dependencies and technical risk across concurrent projects, with clear status communication to all stakeholders
Experience designing lightweight processes that improve execution velocity and accountability in research or engineering environments without adding bureaucratic overhead
Strong analytical skills for evaluating program health, spotting bottlenecks early, and making data-informed prioritization decisions
Excellent communication in both directions: crisp updates for senior leadership, clear direction for researchers
A pragmatic, roll-up-your-sleeves orientation: we need you to be equally effective at the strategic level and in the details
Every team member who works at Deepgram is expected to actively use and experiment with advanced AI tools, and even build your own into your everyday work.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bonus points for experience with speech or audio projects — audio data collection and labeling, audio model evaluation (including human eval), or shipping products where audio quality is the core user experience
ideally with meaningful time spent supporting or embedded in an AI/ML Research organization

scope.surface: Research organization — STT and TTS model development, data pipelines, evaluation infrastructure
scope.business_model: B2B/B2D API platform (cloud APIs and self-hosted/on-premises)
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of model versions and features
Program health and risk visibility to leadership
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end delivery of new model versions and features for flagship STT and TTS offerings
Define and champion lightweight but effective processes for researchers' daily workflows
Map dependencies and risks across all active cross-functional projects
Run cross-functional programs end-to-end: scope definition, timeline management, stakeholder communication, risk mitigation, and launch readiness
Troubleshoot cross-team blockers quickly and build reliable partnerships across the technical organization
Own proactive communication across all levels of the organization
Respond quickly to shifting priorities, translating decisions into clear implications for timelines, team capacity, and dependencies

green_flags: Clear 90-day success criteria described in JD — unusual transparency for expectations
JD is transparent about how it was written (collaborative AI + human process)
Well-defined scope: embedded in Research org with explicit cross-functional partners named
Company has disclosed funding ($215M+, Series C) and notable customer logos
Flexible schedule, unlimited PTO, and generous stipends mentioned
Role appears to have real ownership and impact on flagship products (not support-only)
red_flags: No compensation range stated anywhere in the JD
Location/remote policy not specified — candidates cannot assess eligibility
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed

JD explicitly says 'this may not be the right role if you're not excited to experiment, adapt... or if you're seeking something highly prescriptive with a traditional 9-to-5' — signals potentially high ambiguity and pace
Benefits for non-US employees explicitly noted as variable and potentially lesser, introducing uncertainty for international candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement and scope of ownership)
location.type (not stated in JD; left as 'unclear')
compensation.equity_mentioned (no equity discussed; defaulted to false)
ambiguities: The 'ideally with meaningful time spent supporting or embedded in an AI/ML Research organization' qualifier on the 5+ years requirement is attached to the hard requirement sentence but uses 'ideally', making it semi-soft. Extracted as a separate nice-to-have item to reflect both readings.
Location/remote policy is entirely absent from the JD; location.type set to 'unclear'.
Visa sponsorship and work authorization are not addressed anywhere in the JD.
